description: PII-redacting Anthropic gateway. Every Vibe app routes Claude calls through this to enforce token-vault redaction, audit, quotas, and ZDR.

subdomains.name: gateway.shield
subdomains.emergencyNote: Internal Vibe apps should reach the gateway by service name (http://vibe-shield-gateway:8080) over vibe_net — the emergency port is for cross-host debugging only.

optional.name: ZDR_ENABLED
optional.doc: Anthropic Zero Data Retention. Requires the ZDR addendum signed on the Anthropic account; the header alone does NOT activate ZDR.

ui.label: Anthropic ZDR header
ui.helpText: Sets the anthropic-version + ZDR opt-in header on outbound calls. Requires a signed ZDR addendum with Anthropic — the header alone does nothing without it.

optional.name: RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE
optional.doc: Per-(tenant, app) request cap per minute. Tune via per-tenant policy overrides.

ui.label: Default rate limit (req/min)
ui.helpText: 10–600. Default ceiling for any tenant without a custom policy. Per-tenant policies override this.

optional.name: SPEND_CAP_MICRODOLLARS
optional.doc: Per-tenant monthly spend cap in micro-dollars (USD * 1e6). 500_000_000 = $500/month.

optional.name: ANTHROPIC_REPROBE_INTERVAL_MS
optional.doc: Periodic Anthropic key re-probe interval (ms). 0 disables; default 15 min. Failures log warn + never crash; the next /v1/messages call fails-closed via the existing 401/503 path.

optional.name: MAX_REQUEST_BYTES
optional.doc: Max request body size (bytes). Anthropic accepts up to 256 KB on /v1/messages; default 1 MB here gives headroom for large tool payloads.

optional.name: SESSION_TTL_MINUTES
optional.doc: Session idle TTL (minutes). After this, the token vault discards the per-session DEK and stored tokens become unrecoverable. Shorter = tighter security; longer = better re-id continuity across requests.

optional.name: LOG_LEVEL
optional.doc: Pino log level. fatal | error | warn | info | debug | trace | silent.

firstLogin.note: Vibe Shield admin has no user model — auth is a single X-Admin-Key header. The appliance generates GATEWAY_ADMIN_KEY at enable time and stores it in /opt/vibe/env/vibe-shield.env (mode 600, root-owned). Retrieve via: sudo grep ^GATEWAY_ADMIN_KEY= /opt/vibe/env/vibe-shield.env. Paste the value into the admin login form. The key never lands in browser storage; refresh = re-prompt.
